title: Plan harness-judge LLM-as-judge rubric refinement
description: Plan an iterative refinement of .agents/agents/harness-judge.md and .agents/skills/harness-judge/SKILL.md using local references, web references, Goose compatibility, and open .agents conventions. This bead is planning-only; implementation will happen after synthesis.
design: Use delegates for read-only research: (1) LLM-as-judge literature/web, (2) Goose agents/skills/recipes docs and existing local harness files, (3) local third-party prompt/context/loop/skills/framework/orchestration references. Parent session synthesizes and does not edit files yet.
acceptance_criteria: A synthesized plan exists that maps references to domains A-F; identifies agent-vs-skill content split; lists concrete checklist/question sections to add; notes Goose/open-standard compatibility constraints; and names validation steps before any file edits.
notes:
Synthesis from delegated research (2026-07-18):

Primary design direction:
- Keep .agents/agents/harness-judge.md short: persona, boundaries, when to invoke/not invoke, operating process, output format, and hard safety rules.
- Put the detailed checklists/questions/rubrics/calibration anchors in .agents/skills/harness-judge/SKILL.md.
- Preserve Goose/open .agents compatibility: Markdown + YAML frontmatter; agent name matches file stem; skill name matches directory; skill has metadata.version; recipes validated with goose recipe validate.

Reference-informed judge principles:
- Evidence-first: every score needs artifact path/turn/tool call/quote or explicit "not found".
- Criteria-before-verdict: score individual criteria before aggregate.
- Trajectory-aware: judge ordering, tool calls, delegation, validation, and handoff, not just final answer.
- Bias-aware: guard against verbosity, positional, sycophancy, style/self-enhancement, and circularity.
- Pairwise/layer-delta: score baseline and enhanced independently, then compare criterion-level deltas; allow positive/neutral/negative deltas.
- Reproducible: capture scenario/layer, judge model, rubric version, timestamp, files/transcripts, thresholds.

Domain additions to plan:
A Prompt/context/loop engineering:
- Add judgeability of prompt, context minimality, explicit assumptions, progressive disclosure, loop readiness level, max iterations, pause/kill criteria, escalation, evidence-friendly outputs.
B Skills:
- Add frontmatter/name/path checks, description trigger specificity, progressive disclosure, single responsibility, examples/gotchas/verification, scripts/references/assets separation, observable method steps, layer-delta usefulness.
C Agents:
- Add frontmatter/name/model checks, required sections, invoke/do-not-invoke boundaries, persona specificity, skill-loading expectations, circuit breakers, maker/checker split, no self-approval, no subagent delegation.
D Recipes:
- Add goose recipe validate hard gate, top-level field checks, parameter/subrecipe path checks, AD-001 pattern compliance, eval JSON in-session agents only, methodology locality, completion/handoff conditions.
E Frameworks:
- Add spec-to-test traceability, SDD/TDD sequence evidence, RED-GREEN-REFACTOR, GDD generation constraints/options/tradeoffs, verifier theater red flag, regression evidence.
F Orchestration:
- Add recipe->agent->skill layer discipline, Orchestration decision before delegate, non-overlapping scopes, read-only parallel/write sequenced, context isolation, synthesis not forwarding, delegation audit, circuit breakers.

Concrete sections to add/update later:
- "Common Rationalizations" section: small diff so no tests; env failure so ignore; docs close enough; summoned agents can be listed as in-session; loaded agent vs delegated agent confusion.
- "Red Flags" section: no citations, no validation command, hand-edited generated sections, AD-001 ignored, success claimed after failing validation, verifier theater.
- Numbered checklist IDs: HJ001 evidence cited; HJ002 scope preserved; HJ003 layer comparison correct; HJ004 validation considered; HJ005 generated sections untouched; expand per domain.

Validation plan before accepting edits:
- goose skills list
- python3 scripts/generate-tables.py if generated docs impacted
- python3 scripts/check-consistency.py
- node apps/kg/dist/cli.js pipeline after structural harness changes
- if recipes touched: goose recipe validate relevant file(s)
- optionally eval: node apps/eval-hub/dist/index.js --run --layers agents --subject harness-judge --ambient-goose; and skills --subjects harness-judge.

Important caveat:
- Old Goose docs URLs may redirect/404; prefer current goose-docs.ai docs and local repo invariants.

title: Eval: store Goose session ID — switch from --no-session to named sessions
description:
Currently eval runs use --no-session (ephemeral, no session ID persisted).
We added first_message_id (Anthropic API trace) + token counts as practical alternatives.

To get a real Goose session ID for post-hoc inspection:
1. Replace --no-session with --session eval-{run_id}-{eval_id}-{config}-{run_num}
2. After the run, read the session ID from the goose-home sessions directory
3. Store it in eval_run_results.goose_session_id
4. The session file at ~/.local/state/goose/sessions/ (or isolated goose-home) would then be inspectable with 'goose session list' and 'goose session inspect <id>'

Tradeoff: named sessions persist to disk (more storage), but enable full post-hoc conversation replay.
Current workaround: events.jsonl + first_message_id + token counts cover most debugging needs.
close_reason: Closed
